title: STOP Using Bypass Permissions, Use This New Feature Instead

extracted.hook: Well, I'm back with another Claude code update.
transitions: So, right here you can see that it's able to read things, but now when it wants to actually make changes,
And as you can see, it's popping it up again and stopping my session because it has to make two edits.
So, the fix that I had been using for a long time is within my .claude folder,
But with Claude code's new release of auto mode,
And right here you can see that it says auto mode let's Claude handle permissions automatically.
extracted.cta: "if you learned something new or you enjoyed the video, please give it a like. Definitely helps me out a ton."
extracted.analogies: "So, the fix that I had been using for a long time is within my .claude folder"
curiosity_gaps: "And so, this gave me a lot more control over what Claude code does and what it can't do. And in some cases, I still think that this is the actual best option to use because you can change these per project and you could also set this globally if you'd like"
"So, auto mode is the middle path that lets you run longer tasks with fewer interruptions while introducing less risk than skipping all permissions."

accelerates: discussing the new auto mode feature
slows: explaining the old bypass permissions method in detail
